name: Stream Processing Pipeline with Operators
description: Implement a lazy stream processing pipeline across two modules. Module stream_ops.py must define: Operator abstract base class with abstract method apply(iterable) -> iterable; concrete operators MapOp(fn), FilterOp(predicate), TakeOp(n), SkipOp(n), DistinctOp(), BatchOp(size: int) yields lists, FlattenOp() flattens one level. Module stream.py must import from stream_ops.py and implement Stream class with constructor Stream(source: iterable) and chainable methods map(fn), filter(pred), take(n), skip(n), distinct(), batch(size), flatten() each returning a new Stream; terminal methods to_list(), count() returning int, reduce(fn, initial), first(default=None), for_each(fn). Pipeline is lazy — operators only execute when terminal method is called.
test_imports: from stream_ops import Operator, MapOp, FilterOp, TakeOp, SkipOp, DistinctOp, BatchOp, FlattenOp
signature:
# stream_ops.py
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod

class Operator(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    def apply(self, iterable): ...

class MapOp(Operator): ...
class FilterOp(Operator): ...
class TakeOp(Operator): ...
class SkipOp(Operator): ...
class DistinctOp(Operator): ...
class BatchOp(Operator): ...
class FlattenOp(Operator): ...

# stream.py
from stream_ops import Operator, MapOp, FilterOp, TakeOp, SkipOp, DistinctOp, BatchOp, FlattenOp

class Stream:
    def __init__(self, source): ...
    def map(self, fn) -> 'Stream': ...
    def filter(self, pred) -> 'Stream': ...
    def take(self, n: int) -> 'Stream': ...
    def skip(self, n: int) -> 'Stream': ...
    def distinct(self) -> 'Stream': ...
    def batch(self, size: int) -> 'Stream': ...
    def flatten(self) -> 'Stream': ...
    def to_list(self) -> list: ...
    def count(self) -> int: ...
    def reduce(self, fn, initial): ...
    def first(self, default=None): ...
    def for_each(self, fn) -> None: ...
tests:
def test_map():
    assert Stream([1,2,3]).map(lambda x: x*2).to_list() == [2,4,6]

def test_filter():
    assert Stream([1,2,3,4]).filter(lambda x: x%2==0).to_list() == [2,4]

def test_count():
    assert Stream([1,2,3,4]).filter(lambda x: x>1).count() == 3

def test_reduce():
    assert Stream([1,2,3,4]).reduce(lambda a,b: a+b, 0) == 10

def test_for_each():
    out = []
    Stream([1,2,3]).for_each(out.append)
    assert out == [1,2,3]
constraints: Pipeline is lazy: operators store config but do not iterate until a terminal method runs
Each chainable method returns a new Stream (not mutate)
take and skip never raise on shorter source (just stop early or return [])
batch yields a final partial batch if elements remain
first(default) returns default on empty stream
